items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger` **Service directory:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` **Triggered by:** PR #57869 by `@ryazhang-microsoft` ## What Changed Commit d543f21 added URL-caching to `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` to improve redirect performance for write operations. The policy now caches the latest primary node URL and reuses it for subsequent non-GET requests (CHANGELOG `1.4.1-beta.5`). The `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` itself was introduced in `1.4.1-beta.3` and fixed in `1.4.1-beta.4`. ## Gaps Found - The `## Examples` section in `README.md` only contains the text "Coming Soon..." — there are no real examples at all - `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` is mentioned in the CHANGELOG for beta.3 and beta.5 but never mentioned or described in the README; developers have no way to understand that the SDK automatically follows 307/308 redirects and that write performance is improved through URL caching - The Tags feature code samples in the README use plain `` ```C# `` fences instead of `C# Snippet:` snippet-tool markers, so they are not backed by compiled, test-validated code - `VerifyConnection` defaulting to `true` (fixed in beta.4) is not explained anywhere in the README outside of the CHANGELOG
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` **Action:** Edit 1. Replace the `## Examples` section stub with a real examples section that mirrors the Key Concepts snippets already present (they already have `Snippet:` markers). 2. Add a new sub-section under `## Key concepts` (or a standalone `## Redirect behavior` section) describing `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy`: ```markdown ### Redirect handling The `ConfidentialLedgerClient` automatically follows HTTP 307 and 308 redirects while preserving the `Authorization` header. This is necessary because Azure Confidential Ledger is a distributed system that routes write operations to the primary node; non-primary nodes respond with a redirect. The SDK also caches the latest primary node URL obtained from redirect responses and reuses it for subsequent non-GET requests, reducing the number of round-trips for write-heavy workloads. No additional configuration is required to enable this behavior. ``` 3. Replace the two inline Tags code blocks (which use plain `C#` fences) with properly named snippet placeholders: For the submit-with-tags block, add a `C# Snippet:CreateLedgerEntryWithTags` placeholder. For the get-with-tags block, add a `C# Snippet:GetLedgerEntriesWithTags` placeholder. 4. Add a note about `VerifyConnection` under the `### Authenticate the client` section: ```markdown > **Note:** By default, `VerifyConnection` is `true` in `ConfidentialLedgerClientOptions`, which means the SDK validates the ledger's TLS certificate against the trusted identity service certificate. Set `VerifyConnection = false` only for development or testing scenarios. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The Tags feature code blocks must be backed by compiled test code. The existing sample files are under `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/`. 1. Open or create `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/Sample_Tags.cs` 2. Add two methods with the following structure: ```csharp // #region Snippet:CreateLedgerEntryWithTags string tags = "tag1,tag2"; Response result = await Client.CreateLedgerEntryAsync(content, collectionId, tags); // #endregion // #region Snippet:GetLedgerEntriesWithTags // Only one tag is permitted per retrieval operation. var result = Client.GetLedgerEntriesAsync(collectionId, "tag1"); // #endregion ``` 3. In `README.md`, replace the plain C# blocks for Tags with: ````markdown ```C# Snippet:CreateLedgerEntryWithTags ``` ```` and ````markdown ```C# Snippet:GetLedgerEntriesWithTags ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently has these sections: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ❌ Examples — currently says "Coming Soon…"; must be populated 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing **Action for Examples section:** The existing Key Concepts section already contains the primary usage snippets (`AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, `GetReceipt`, etc.). The Examples section should link to those or include a summary with a pointer to the `tests/samples/` directory. Alternatively, extract the most common end-to-end flow (create entry → check status → read entry) into an Examples snippet. Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` — confirms README placeholders are filled from test regions 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 confidentialledger` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [ConfidentialLedger] Docs: Update README with Examples, RedirectPolicy, and Tags snippet coverage
